concerns.text: FR-013 contains corrupted URL for KnotInfo reference validation: '( nodename nor servname provided, or not known)'))])'. Constitution Principle II (Verified Accuracy) requires all external citations to be verifiable against primary sources. This blocks verification of the validation approach, making the hyperbolic volume validation requirement untestable. Fix by providing valid KnotInfo URL or removing the citation if no verifiable reference exists.

FR-013 specifies validation against KnotInfo but does not quantify expected reference coverage. If KnotInfo coverage <90%, validation is skipped per the threshold rule. Without estimating expected coverage, it's unclear whether this validation step will be actionable or will always trigger the skip condition. Add feasibility assessment or adjust threshold to match expected coverage.

Spec acknowledges census data makes p-values inapplicable, yet FR-006 still requires reporting p-values 'for convention.' This creates methodological confusion: if p-values have no inferential meaning, why document them? Clarify whether p-values will be omitted entirely or explicitly marked as 'not applicable for census data' in all outputs to avoid misinterpretation.

FR-013 contains corrupted URL text for KnotInfo reference values: '( nodename nor servname provided, or not known)'))])' instead of a valid citation. This blocks Reference-Validator verification per Constitution Principle II (Verified Accuracy). The URL must be corrected to enable citation validation.

FR-013 contains corrupted KnotInfo URL: '( nodename nor servname provided, or not known)'))])'. This blocks Reference-Validator verification per Constitution Principle II (Verified Accuracy). Replace with valid KnotInfo URL (https://knotinfo.math.indiana.edu) to enable citation verification.

FR-013 contains corrupted URL text: '( nodename nor servname provided, or not known)'))])' instead of valid KnotInfo URL. This blocks Constitution Principle II (Verified Accuracy) verification per Reference-Validator Agent requirements. Previous revision attempts failed to fix this.

FR-013's KnotInfo citation is malformed and cannot be verified by Reference-Validator Agent. The URL must be corrected to a valid KnotInfo reference URL (e.g., https://knotinfo.math.indiana.edu/) before the Verified Accuracy Gate can pass.

InvariantsDataset contract fields (dataset_id, data_source, created_at, crossing_number_range, validated_range, total_records, hyperbolic_records, null_percentage_*) do not match data-model.md fields (records, download_timestamp, source_version, validation_scope). Contract and data model must align for consistency.

RegressionModel contract requires model_id, target, fitted_timestamp, random_seed, census_data_flag, p_value_disclaimer which are not defined in data-model.md's RegressionModel entity. Contract schema extends data model without documentation of the extension.

FR-013 contains corrupted URL text '( nodename nor servname provided, or not known)'))]' for KnotInfo reference. This blocks validation strategy from being executed as designed. Validation against KnotInfo is central to the hyperbolic volume accuracy claim. Without a valid reference source, the validation requirement is untestable.

Using crossing number and braid index as joint predictors where braid index ≤ crossing number is a known mathematical constraint creates multicollinearity by definition. While VIF assessment is planned, the regression analysis cannot establish independent explanatory power - only variance partitioning within the census. This must be explicitly stated as a limitation on what the regression can claim.

Validation of Knot Atlas hyperbolic volume against KnotInfo may be circular if both databases derive from common sources (Hoste-Thistlethwaite-Weeks enumeration). FR-013 acknowledges this but the validation strategy does not account for non-independence of validation targets from predictors. This limits the strength of accuracy claims.

For census data (all prime knots ≤13 crossings), p-values are documented 'for convention only' but FR-006 still requires reporting p-values alongside effect sizes. This creates potential for misinterpretation. The plan should explicitly suppress p-value reporting entirely for census data rather than documenting them with disclaimers.

FR-013 still contains corrupted URL for KnotInfo: '( nodename nor servname provided, or not known)'))])'. Reviser fixed research.md but spec.md remains unchanged. Constitution Principle II (Verified Accuracy) requires all external citations to be verifiable. This blocks verification of the validation approach.

FR-013 validation threshold still says 'high match threshold' without quantification. research.md specifies ≥50% but spec.md lacks this explicit threshold. Inconsistency between spec and research artifacts creates testability ambiguity.

FR-006 still states 'p-values are documented for completeness per convention' while research.md says p-values were removed. Spec should match research on whether p-values appear in outputs. Inconsistent guidance creates implementation ambiguity.
